description: Implement the core turn-based combat system: action points, melee attacks, ranged attacks with ammo, hit/miss calculation, damage dealing, and entity death. This makes the game playable as a combat roguelike.
acceptance_criteria: Each entity (player and enemies) has an AP pool that refreshes at the start of their turn.
Given the player is adjacent to an enemy, when they press the attack key or move into the enemy tile, then a melee attack is executed costing 1 AP.
Given the player has a ranged weapon equipped with ammo > 0, when they target an enemy in LOS and press fire, then a ranged attack is executed, deducting 1 ammo.
Hit chance is calculated based on distance, cover status, and weapon accuracy — displayed as a percentage before confirming the attack.
Damage reduces the target's HP. When HP reaches 0, the entity is removed from the grid and a death animation/effect plays.
A combat log message is appended to the message log for every attack (hit/miss, damage dealt, entity killed).
The player can see their own HP, current weapon, and ammo count in the HUD at all times.
spec_section: 5.1 Turn-Based Grid Combat, 5.1 Ranged vs. Melee
notes: Spec: 'Each action (move, attack, use item, interact) costs AP. Enemies act simultaneously after the player commits their turn.' Keep hit calculation simple for MVP — distance + cover modifier.
